text: Children of Turkish origin constitute one of the largest immigrant groups in the European countries of Austria, France and Sweden. Despite their large numbers, members of this group are exposed to many forms of discrimination and blockages in their striving for social advancement – especially within the educational system. The studies presented in this volume make clear that discrimination and obstacles also affect children of Turkish origin in these countries – one of the largest immigrant groups in Europe. A common theme of the studies is that of administrative conditions that affect the destiny of the second generation. The educational system of the countries in question also differs markedly and the countries with more favourable administrative conditions for the educational system have had a more favourable effect on the chances of economic advancement for the second generation.
